sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = history[-2].frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Changes after clicking button row 33, col 45 ===")
for r in range(len(before)):
    if before[r] != after[r]:
        print(f"Row {r}:")
        print(f"  Before: {before[r]}")
        print(f"  After:  {after[r]}")

# Check right panel button states
print("\n=== Current right panel button states ===")
for r in [33, 36, 39, 42, 45, 48]:
    print(f"  Row {r}: {after[r][37:58]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Good, I can see that clicking row 33, col 45 toggled the button at column 2 (col 44-46) from www to BBB. So the buttons toggle between www (gray) and BBB (black).
Now I see the right panel buttons:
- Row 33: Wwww WWBBB WWwww WWwww WW - column 2 is BBB (toggled)
- Row 36: WWw WWWWw WWWWw WWWWw WWW
- Row 39: Wwww WWwww WWwww WWwww WW
- Row 42: WWw WWWWw WWWWw WWWWw WWW
- Row 45: Wwww WWwww WWwww WWwww WW
- Row 48: WWw WWWWw WWWWw WWWWw WWW
The button at col 44-46, row 33 is now BBB. This is the horizontal bar for column 2.
Let me think about what the buttons represent. The right panel has 4 columns and 6 rows (3 horizontal + 3 vertical). The upper yellow object is at rows 7-11, cols 44-49.
The upper yellow object shape:
- Row 7: cols 44-49 (horizontal bar)
- Row 8: cols 44-45 (vertical bar left), cols 48-49 (vertical bar right)
- Row 9: cols 44-45, cols 48-49
- Row 10: cols 44-45, cols 48-49
- Row 11: cols 44-45, cols 48-49
Wait, that doesn't look right. Let me check the actual shape.
Actually, looking at the image, the upper yellow object is a U-shape:
- Top horizontal bar
- Two vertical bars going down
The lower yellow object is a block:
- A 2x2 or 2x3 block
Let me look at the segmentation data to understand the shapes better.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me look at what the buttons correspond to. The right panel has 4 columns and 6 rows. The buttons might correspond to the cells of a 4x6 grid that represents the object's shape.
The upper yellow object spans rows 7-11 (5 rows) and cols 44-49 (6 cols). The lower yellow object spans rows 24-27 (4 rows) and cols 45-48 (4 cols).
Hmm, the button grid is 4x6 (4 columns, 6 rows) but the objects have different dimensions.
Wait, maybe the buttons don't directly correspond to the object cells. Maybe they represent something else.
Let me try clicking more buttons and see if the score increases. In level 1, each button click increased the score by 1. Let me see if the same happens here.
Actually, I notice the score hasn't changed since level 2 started. Let me check if clicking the buttons increases the score.
